quiz.q: Why does a negative discriminant indicate that a quadratic equation has no real roots?
options: A reason unrelated to the discriminant
A negative discriminant always indicates two real, distinct roots
The discriminant’s sign has no connection to the type of roots a quadratic has
A negative value under the square root in the quadratic formula produces non-real, complex results

quiz.q: Why is the discriminant a useful tool before fully solving a quadratic equation?
options: A reason unrelated to solving quadratic equations
It allows a student to quickly determine the number and type of roots without applying the entire quadratic formula
The discriminant provides no useful information about a quadratic equation
Calculating the discriminant always takes longer than fully solving the equation

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Chemistry: Periodic Trends
subjects.summary: Grade 10 Chemistry strand (extension): periodic trends describe predictable patterns in properties such as atomic radius, ionization energy, and electronegativity as one moves across a period or down a group of the periodic table.

quiz.q: Periodic trends describe predictable patterns in properties as one moves across a ___ of the periodic table.
options: A concept unrelated to the periodic table
Colour scheme only, with no connection to chemical properties
Random arrangement with no predictable pattern
Period or down a group

quiz.q: As you move from left to right across a period, atomic radius generally ___.
options: Stays exactly the same
Increases
A trend unrelated to atomic radius
Decreases

quiz.q: As you move down a group of the periodic table, atomic radius generally ___.
options: Decreases
Increases
A trend unrelated to atomic radius
Stays exactly the same

quiz.q: Why does ionization energy generally increase as you move from left to right across a period?
options: Increasing nuclear charge pulls the outermost electrons more tightly, making them harder to remove
Ionization energy always decreases across a period, with no connection to nuclear charge
Nuclear charge has no effect on how tightly electrons are held
A reason unrelated to periodic trends

quiz.q: Why are periodic trends useful for predicting how an element will behave in a chemical reaction?
options: A reason unrelated to periodic trends
They provide insight into an atom’s tendency to gain, lose, or share electrons based on its position on the periodic table
Periodic trends provide no useful information about chemical behaviour
An element’s position on the periodic table never affects how it reacts

subjects.subject: History
subjects.title: Canada and the First World War: Vimy Ridge and the Birth of National Identity
subjects.summary: Grade 10 History strand: Canada entered the First World War in 1914 as part of the British Empire, and the 1917 victory at Vimy Ridge, where Canadian troops fought together as a unified force for the first time, became a defining symbol of emerging Canadian national identity.

quiz.q: Canada entered the First World War in which year?
At the Battle of Vimy Ridge, Canadian troops fought together as a unified force for the ___.
options: First time
Last time before Confederation
A detail unrelated to Vimy Ridge
Only time in Canadian military history

quiz.q: Canada’s participation in the First World War was initially tied to its status as part of ___.
options: An independent nation with no ties to Britain
A relationship unrelated to Canada’s WWI involvement
The British Empire
The United States’ military alliance

quiz.q: Why is the Battle of Vimy Ridge often described as a defining moment in Canadian national identity?
options: Canadian troops’ success fighting as a unified national force helped foster a growing sense of pride and distinct identity
This topic has no relevance to understanding Canadian history
This battle had no connection to Canadian national identity
Vimy Ridge has no lasting significance in Canadian history

quiz.q: Why do historians often connect Canada’s First World War experience to its later push for greater independence from Britain?
options: Canada’s First World War experience had no connection to its later independence
Canadian independence was never connected to any historical events
The war’s sacrifices and achievements strengthened Canada’s confidence in asserting its own voice on the world stage
This topic has no relevance to understanding Canadian history

subjects.subject: English
subjects.title: Reading: Motif and Recurring Imagery
subjects.summary: Grade 10 English strand: a motif is a recurring element, image, or idea that appears throughout a text, reinforcing a story’s themes and adding layers of meaning through repetition.

quiz.q: A motif is best described as a recurring ___.
options: Element, image, or idea that appears throughout a text
Character who appears only once in a story
Setting that changes completely with every scene
A concept unrelated to literary analysis

quiz.q: Why might an author repeat a specific image throughout a novel?
options: A reason unrelated to literary technique
Repetition can reinforce a theme or draw attention to its significance
Repeating an image always weakens a story’s structure
Authors never intentionally repeat images or ideas

quiz.q: Which is an example of how a motif might function in a text?
options: An element that never connects to any broader theme
A recurring symbol, such as light and darkness, that reinforces a central theme
A technique unrelated to motif
A single unrelated detail mentioned only once

quiz.q: Why might tracking a motif across an entire novel deepen a reader’s understanding of theme?
options: Motifs never change or develop throughout a text
Seeing how a motif evolves or shifts can reveal how a theme develops over the course of the story
A reason unrelated to thematic analysis
Tracking a motif provides no additional insight into a text

quiz.q: Why is motif considered distinct from a one-time literary device, like a single metaphor?
options: This distinction has no connection to literary analysis
A motif and a single metaphor are always identical in function
A motif recurs multiple times across a text, building cumulative meaning, rather than appearing only once
Motifs only ever appear a single time in a text

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Algebra: Direct and Partial Variation
subjects.summary: Grade 10 Algebra strand (extension): direct variation describes a relationship where one quantity is a constant multiple of another, expressed as y = kx, while partial variation includes an additional constant term, expressed as y = kx + b.

quiz.q: Direct variation describes a relationship expressed in the form ___.
options: y = k divided by x
y = kx
An expression unrelated to direct variation
y = kx + b

quiz.q: Partial variation differs from direct variation because it includes ___.
options: A difference unrelated to variation relationships
An additional constant term
A variable exponent on x
No constant of variation at all

quiz.q: In a direct variation relationship, when x = 0, the value of y must be ___.
options: A value unrelated to direct variation
Undefined in every case
Equal to the constant of variation, k

quiz.q: Why does the graph of a partial variation relationship not pass through the origin, unlike a direct variation relationship?
options: The constant of variation, k, has no effect on where a line is positioned
A reason unrelated to variation relationships
The added constant term shifts the line vertically away from the point (0, 0)
Partial variation relationships always pass through the origin, just like direct variation

quiz.q: Why are direct and partial variation useful for modelling real-world relationships, such as the cost of a service with a flat fee plus a per-unit rate?
options: Costs involving a flat fee and a per-unit rate can never be modelled using these relationships
A reason unrelated to variation relationships
Direct and partial variation have no real-world modelling applications
They provide a simple linear model that reflects either a purely proportional relationship or one with a fixed starting value

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Biology: The Circulatory and Respiratory Systems
subjects.summary: Grade 10 Biology strand: the circulatory system transports oxygen, nutrients, and waste throughout the body using the heart and blood vessels, working closely with the respiratory system, which exchanges oxygen and carbon dioxide between the body and the environment.

quiz.q: The circulatory system primarily transports oxygen, nutrients, and waste using the heart and ___.
options: A structure unrelated to circulation
Digestive organs only, with no connection to blood
Blood vessels
Lungs exclusively, with no connection to blood vessels

quiz.q: The respiratory system primarily exchanges which two gases between the body and the environment?
options: A pair of gases unrelated to respiration
Oxygen and helium
Nitrogen and hydrogen
Oxygen and carbon dioxide

quiz.q: Which structure in the circulatory system pumps blood throughout the body?
options: A structure unrelated to circulation
The heart
The diaphragm
The lungs

quiz.q: Why do the circulatory and respiratory systems work closely together?
options: A reason unrelated to human body systems
The respiratory system has no role in delivering oxygen to the body’s cells
These two systems function completely independently with no connection to each other
The circulatory system carries the oxygen absorbed by the respiratory system to cells throughout the body, and returns carbon dioxide for exhalation

quiz.q: Why is understanding the circulatory and respiratory systems important for studying conditions like cardiovascular disease?
options: Cardiovascular disease has no connection to the circulatory system
A reason unrelated to human health
These body systems have no connection to any medical conditions
Many health conditions arise from disruptions to how these systems transport oxygen and maintain blood flow

subjects.subject: History
subjects.title: The Conscription Crisis of 1917
subjects.summary: Grade 10 History strand: the 1917 Conscription Crisis erupted when the federal government introduced mandatory military service to address wartime troop shortages, a decision that deeply divided English and French Canada and strained national unity.

quiz.q: The Conscription Crisis took place in which year?
Conscription refers to a policy of ___.
options: A policy unrelated to military service
Mandatory civilian labour with no connection to the military
Voluntary military enlistment only
Mandatory military service

quiz.q: The federal government introduced conscription in 1917 primarily to address ___.
options: A shortage of agricultural labourers
A reason entirely unrelated to the war
Wartime troop shortages
A shortage of factory workers

quiz.q: Why did the Conscription Crisis deeply divide English and French Canada?
options: Many French Canadians felt less connected to a war fought primarily for British imperial interests, while many English Canadians strongly supported the war effort
This topic has no relevance to understanding Canadian history
This crisis had no connection to any divisions within Canada
English and French Canadians held identical views on conscription

quiz.q: Why do historians consider the Conscription Crisis an important moment in Canadian political history?
options: It revealed and deepened long-standing tensions between English and French Canada that would continue to shape national politics
This topic has no relevance to understanding Canadian history
English and French Canada have never experienced any political tension
The Conscription Crisis had no lasting effect on Canadian politics

subjects.subject: English
subjects.title: Grammar: Sentence Fragments and Run-On Sentences
subjects.summary: Grade 10 English strand: a sentence fragment is an incomplete sentence missing a subject, verb, or complete thought, while a run-on sentence improperly joins two or more independent clauses without correct punctuation or conjunctions.

quiz.q: A sentence fragment is best described as a group of words that ___.
options: Is missing a subject, verb, or complete thought
Is always too long to be considered a single sentence
Always contains two complete independent clauses
A concept unrelated to sentence structure

quiz.q: A run-on sentence occurs when ___.
options: Two or more independent clauses are joined without correct punctuation or conjunctions
A sentence contains only a single word
A concept unrelated to grammar
A sentence is missing its subject entirely

quiz.q: Which of these is an example of a sentence fragment?
options: After she finished, she ran through the park.
A sentence unrelated to fragments or run-ons
She ran through the park every morning.
Running through the park every morning.

quiz.q: Why might a writer use a semicolon to correct a run-on sentence?
options: A reason unrelated to correcting run-on sentences
A semicolon can never be used to join two clauses
A semicolon can properly join two closely related independent clauses without a coordinating conjunction
Semicolons always create a sentence fragment instead of fixing one

quiz.q: Why is it important to avoid both fragments and run-ons in formal academic writing?
options: Formal writing has no expectations around sentence structure
These errors never affect how clearly a sentence communicates meaning
A reason unrelated to writing conventions
Both errors can confuse a reader and obscure the intended meaning of a sentence

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Financial Literacy: Compound Interest and Amortization
subjects.summary: Grade 10 Financial Literacy strand (extension): compound interest is calculated on both the initial principal and the accumulated interest from previous periods, and an amortization schedule breaks down how each loan payment is divided between interest and principal over time.

quiz.q: Compound interest is calculated based on ___.
options: Both the initial principal and the accumulated interest from previous periods
Only the initial principal, with no connection to accumulated interest
A fixed amount that never changes over time
A concept unrelated to interest calculations

quiz.q: An amortization schedule shows how each loan payment is divided between ___.
options: Taxes and fees exclusively
A concept unrelated to loan repayment
Interest and principal
Only interest, with no connection to principal

quiz.q: Compared to simple interest, compound interest generally causes an investment to grow ___.
options: At exactly the same rate as simple interest
A comparison unrelated to interest calculations
Faster over time
Slower over time

quiz.q: Why does the portion of an amortized loan payment applied to interest typically decrease over the life of the loan?
options: A reason unrelated to amortization
As the principal balance shrinks with each payment, the interest charged on that smaller balance also decreases
The amount applied to interest never changes throughout a loan’s term
The portion applied to interest always increases over the life of the loan

quiz.q: Why is understanding compound interest important when comparing long-term savings or investment options?
options: Small differences in interest rate or compounding frequency can lead to significantly different amounts over a long period of time
All savings and investment options grow at an identical rate regardless of compounding
A reason unrelated to financial literacy
Compound interest has no meaningful effect on long-term savings or investments

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Physics: Static Electricity and Electric Charge
subjects.summary: Grade 10 Physics strand: static electricity results from an imbalance of electric charge on an object’s surface, typically caused by the transfer of electrons through friction, and objects with like charges repel while objects with opposite charges attract.

quiz.q: Static electricity results from an imbalance of ___ on an object’s surface.
options: Temperature
Electric charge
Mass
A concept unrelated to static electricity

quiz.q: Static electricity is typically caused by the transfer of ___ through friction.
options: A particle unrelated to static electricity
Neutrons
Protons
Electrons

quiz.q: Objects with like electric charges will ___ each other.
options: Repel
A result unrelated to electric charge
Have no effect on
Attract

quiz.q: Why does rubbing two different materials together often generate static electricity?
options: Rubbing two materials together always removes all electric charge from both objects
Friction never causes any transfer of electric charge between materials
A reason unrelated to static electricity
The friction can transfer electrons from one material to the other, creating a charge imbalance

quiz.q: Why is understanding static electricity important for practical safety concerns, such as around flammable materials?
options: A reason unrelated to physics
Static discharges are never capable of producing a spark
A static discharge can create a spark capable of igniting flammable vapours or materials
Static electricity has no connection to any safety concerns

subjects.subject: History
subjects.title: Growing Canadian Autonomy: The Chanak Affair and the Statute of Westminster
subjects.summary: Grade 10 History strand: Canada’s refusal to automatically support Britain during the 1922 Chanak Affair and the 1931 Statute of Westminster, which granted Canada full legal independence in domestic and foreign affairs, marked key steps in Canada’s growing autonomy from Britain.

quiz.q: The Chanak Affair took place in which year?
The Statute of Westminster was passed in which year?
The Statute of Westminster granted Canada ___.
options: Increased military obligations to Britain
Full legal independence in domestic and foreign affairs
A result unrelated to Canadian autonomy
Complete separation from the British monarchy entirely

quiz.q: Why is Canada’s response to the Chanak Affair considered an early sign of growing Canadian autonomy?
options: The Chanak Affair had no connection to Canadian autonomy
Canada immediately and fully supported Britain’s request with no hesitation
This topic has no relevance to understanding Canadian history
Canada declined to automatically commit troops to support Britain, showing a new willingness to make independent foreign policy decisions

quiz.q: Why do historians view the Statute of Westminster as a major turning point in Canada’s relationship with Britain?
options: It formally recognized Canada’s legal authority to govern itself independently, rather than remaining subordinate to British parliamentary decisions
The Statute of Westminster had no effect on Canada’s relationship with Britain
This topic has no relevance to understanding Canadian history
This legislation increased Britain’s control over Canadian affairs

subjects.subject: English
subjects.title: Writing: Analyzing Diction and Tone
subjects.summary: Grade 10 English strand: diction refers to an author’s specific word choices, while tone is the author’s attitude toward a subject conveyed through that diction, style, and other stylistic choices.

quiz.q: Diction refers to an author’s ___.
options: Specific word choices
Choice of setting exclusively, with no connection to word choice
Handwriting or font style alone
A concept unrelated to writing style

quiz.q: Tone is best described as ___.
options: A synonym for a story’s plot events
A concept unrelated to diction
The physical setting of a narrative, with no connection to attitude
The author’s attitude toward a subject, conveyed through word choice and style

quiz.q: Which is an example of how diction can shape tone?
options: A technique unrelated to diction and tone
Word choice never has any effect on a text’s tone
Diction only affects a text’s length, not its tone
Choosing harsh, blunt words rather than gentle, soft ones to create a critical tone

quiz.q: Why might two authors describe the same event using very different diction?
options: Word choice has no connection to an author’s perspective
Each author’s specific word choices reflect their own distinct attitude or perspective toward the event
A reason unrelated to diction and tone
Diction is always identical across every author who writes about the same event

quiz.q: Why is analyzing diction and tone considered a valuable close-reading skill?
options: Only plot events, not word choice, ever reveal an author’s attitude
This skill has no connection to understanding a text more deeply
It allows readers to uncover an author’s underlying attitude or perspective beyond the literal meaning of the words
Diction and tone are never worth analyzing closely

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Geometry: Angle of Elevation and Depression
subjects.summary: Grade 10 Trigonometry strand: the angle of elevation is the angle measured upward from a horizontal line of sight to an object above, while the angle of depression is the angle measured downward from a horizontal line of sight to an object below.

quiz.q: The angle of elevation is measured ___ from a horizontal line of sight.
options: Downward, to an object below
A concept unrelated to angles of elevation
Upward, to an object above
Sideways, with no vertical component

quiz.q: The angle of depression is measured ___ from a horizontal line of sight.
options: Downward, to an object below
Upward, to an object above
A concept unrelated to angles of depression
Only along the ground, with no connection to height

quiz.q: If a person on top of a cliff looks down at a boat, the angle formed between their horizontal line of sight and the boat is called the ___.
options: A right angle by definition, with no need for measurement
Angle of depression
An angle unrelated to this scenario
Angle of elevation

quiz.q: Why are the angle of elevation and the angle of depression between two points always equal to one another?
options: The angle of depression is always exactly double the angle of elevation
A reason unrelated to angles of elevation and depression
These two angles are never related to each other in any way
They are alternate interior angles formed by a transversal crossing two parallel horizontal lines of sight

quiz.q: Why are angle of elevation and depression problems useful for solving real-world measurement tasks, such as finding the height of a building?
options: A reason unrelated to trigonometry
These angles have no real-world measurement applications
They allow trigonometric ratios to be applied to indirectly calculate distances or heights that cannot be measured directly
Heights of buildings can never be calculated using angles of elevation or depression

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Earth Science: Renewable and Non-Renewable Energy Resources
subjects.summary: Grade 10 Earth Science strand: renewable energy resources, such as solar and wind, can be replenished naturally within a short time frame, while non-renewable energy resources, such as coal and oil, take millions of years to form and are being depleted faster than they can be replaced.

quiz.q: Renewable energy resources are best described as resources that ___.
options: Can be replenished naturally within a short time frame
Can never be replenished under any circumstances
Take millions of years to form
A concept unrelated to energy resources

quiz.q: Which of these is an example of a non-renewable energy resource?
options: Coal
Solar energy
An energy source unrelated to this classification
Wind energy

quiz.q: Non-renewable energy resources are generally being used ___ than they can naturally be replaced.
options: Faster
At exactly the same rate
Slower
A comparison unrelated to energy resources

quiz.q: Why are fossil fuels classified as non-renewable energy resources?
options: This classification has no connection to how quickly a resource forms
Fossil fuels can be replenished within a single human lifetime
A reason unrelated to energy classification
They take millions of years to form from ancient organic matter, far longer than the rate at which they are consumed

quiz.q: Why is the shift toward renewable energy resources considered important for long-term sustainability?
options: Non-renewable energy resources will never become depleted
Renewable energy resources have no connection to long-term sustainability
A reason unrelated to Earth Science
Renewable resources can be replenished naturally, reducing the risk of depleting a limited, non-renewable supply

subjects.subject: History
subjects.title: The 1918-1920 Spanish Flu Pandemic in Canada
subjects.summary: Grade 10 History strand: the Spanish Flu pandemic swept through Canada from 1918 to 1920, killing tens of thousands of people, overwhelming healthcare systems already strained by the First World War, and prompting new public health measures.

quiz.q: The Spanish Flu pandemic affected Canada primarily during which years?
The Spanish Flu pandemic arrived in Canada around the same time as ___.
options: The end of the First World War
An event entirely unrelated to the pandemic’s timing
The Second World War
The Great Depression

quiz.q: The Spanish Flu pandemic in Canada is estimated to have caused ___.
options: A death toll unrelated to public health history
Tens of thousands of deaths
No deaths at all
Fewer than one hundred deaths nationwide

quiz.q: Why did the Spanish Flu pandemic place such severe strain on Canadian healthcare systems at the time?
options: This pandemic had no connection to the state of healthcare in Canada
Canadian healthcare systems faced no additional strain during this period
Healthcare resources and personnel were already stretched thin by the ongoing demands of the First World War
This topic has no relevance to understanding Canadian history

quiz.q: Why do historians and public health officials continue to study the 1918-1920 pandemic today?
options: This topic has no relevance to understanding Canadian history
This event has been entirely forgotten with no historical record
It offers important lessons about public health responses, healthcare capacity, and the spread of infectious disease
The Spanish Flu pandemic has no lasting relevance to public health today

subjects.subject: English
subjects.title: Writing: The Cover Letter and Resume
subjects.summary: Grade 10 English strand: a cover letter introduces a candidate and explains their interest in a specific opportunity, while a resume summarizes a candidate’s skills, education, and experience in a clear, organized format.

quiz.q: A cover letter is primarily used to ___.
options: Introduce a candidate and explain their interest in a specific opportunity
Replace the need for a resume entirely
A concept unrelated to professional writing
Provide a complete, detailed list of every job a candidate has ever held

quiz.q: A resume is best described as a document that ___.
options: Tells a detailed personal story with no organization
Summarizes a candidate’s skills, education, and experience in a clear, organized format
A concept unrelated to professional writing
Is always written in the same format as a cover letter

quiz.q: Why should a cover letter be tailored to a specific job rather than written generically?
options: Cover letters should never mention a specific job or opportunity
A reason unrelated to professional writing
A generic cover letter is always more effective than a tailored one
A tailored letter can directly connect a candidate’s relevant skills to the specific opportunity being sought

quiz.q: Why is clear, concise formatting especially important in a resume?
options: It allows a reader to quickly scan and identify a candidate’s most relevant qualifications
A reason unrelated to professional writing
Resumes are always read in extremely close detail, word for word
Formatting has no effect on how a resume is read or understood

quiz.q: Why are cover letter and resume writing considered valuable skills to develop in Grade 10?
options: These skills have no real-world application for students
A reason unrelated to professional or practical writing
Only adults with careers ever need to write a cover letter or resume
They prepare students for real-world tasks like applying for jobs, volunteer positions, or programs

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Geometry: The Pythagorean Theorem in Three Dimensions
subjects.summary: Grade 10 Geometry strand (extension): the Pythagorean theorem can be extended into three dimensions to find the diagonal length of a rectangular prism by applying the relationship twice, first to a base rectangle and then to the resulting right triangle.

quiz.q: In three dimensions, the Pythagorean theorem can be used to find the ___ of a rectangular prism.
options: Surface area
Volume
Diagonal length
A measurement unrelated to the Pythagorean theorem

quiz.q: To find the diagonal of a rectangular prism, the Pythagorean theorem is generally applied ___.
options: Only once, with no additional steps required
Zero times, since this shape has no diagonal
Twice, first to a base rectangle and then to the resulting right triangle
A method unrelated to this calculation

quiz.q: For a rectangular prism with length l, width w, and height h, the length of the space diagonal, d, satisfies ___.
options: A relationship unrelated to this calculation
d = l + w + h
d² = l² + w² + h²
d² = l² - w² - h²

quiz.q: Why must the diagonal of the base rectangle be calculated as an intermediate step when finding a rectangular prism’s space diagonal?
options: The base diagonal has no connection to the three-dimensional diagonal
This intermediate step is unnecessary and can always be skipped
A reason unrelated to this calculation
That base diagonal becomes one leg of the right triangle used to calculate the full three-dimensional diagonal

quiz.q: Why is extending the Pythagorean theorem into three dimensions useful in fields like construction or design?
options: The Pythagorean theorem can never be applied to three-dimensional shapes
A reason unrelated to three-dimensional geometry
It allows the direct distance between opposite corners of a three-dimensional object to be calculated without physical measurement
This extension has no real-world application in construction or design

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Chemistry: Empirical and Molecular Formulas
subjects.summary: Grade 10 Chemistry strand (extension): an empirical formula shows the simplest whole-number ratio of atoms in a compound, while a molecular formula shows the actual number of atoms of each element present in a single molecule.

quiz.q: An empirical formula shows the ___ of atoms in a compound.
options: Mass in grams
Simplest whole-number ratio
A concept unrelated to chemical formulas
Exact total number, regardless of ratio

quiz.q: A molecular formula shows ___.
options: The charge of the overall compound only
A concept unrelated to chemical formulas
Only the ratio of atoms, with no connection to actual quantity
The actual number of atoms of each element in a single molecule

quiz.q: If a compound has the molecular formula C6H12O6, what would its empirical formula be?
options: A formula unrelated to this compound
CH2O
C3H6O3
C6H12O6

quiz.q: Why can two different compounds share the same empirical formula but have different molecular formulas?
options: A reason unrelated to chemical formulas
Their molecular formulas can be different whole-number multiples of the same simplest ratio
Empirical and molecular formulas are always identical for every compound
Two compounds can never share the same empirical formula

quiz.q: Why is determining a compound’s empirical formula a useful step in identifying an unknown substance?
options: It provides the simplest ratio of elements present, which can then be used alongside molar mass data to determine the full molecular formula
An unknown substance can never be identified using its empirical formula
Empirical formulas provide no useful information for identifying a compound
A reason unrelated to chemical analysis

subjects.subject: History
subjects.title: The Bomarc Missile Crisis and Canada-US Cold War Tensions
subjects.summary: Grade 10 History strand: the early 1960s Bomarc missile controversy, over whether Canada should accept nuclear warheads for American-designed missiles on Canadian soil, exposed deep divisions within Canadian politics and ultimately contributed to the fall of the Diefenbaker government.

quiz.q: The Bomarc missile controversy took place primarily during which decade?
options: The 1940s
The 1980s
The 1920s
The 1960s

quiz.q: The Bomarc missile controversy centred on whether Canada should accept ___.
options: A trade agreement entirely unrelated to defense
Nuclear warheads for American-designed missiles on Canadian soil
A controversy unrelated to Cold War defense policy
New immigration quotas from the United States

quiz.q: The Bomarc missile controversy contributed to the fall of which prime minister’s government?
options: A leader unrelated to this controversy
Lester Pearson
Pierre Trudeau
John Diefenbaker

quiz.q: Why did the Bomarc missile controversy expose deep divisions within Canadian politics?
options: This controversy had no connection to any political divisions
This topic has no relevance to understanding Canadian history
It raised difficult questions about Canadian sovereignty, nuclear weapons, and the country’s military relationship with the United States
All Canadian politicians agreed completely on this issue

quiz.q: Why do historians study the Bomarc missile crisis as part of Canada’s broader Cold War history?
options: The Bomarc crisis has no connection to Canada’s Cold War history
Canada’s defense policy was never influenced by Cold War tensions
It illustrates the pressures Canada faced in balancing its own sovereignty with its defense commitments to the United States during the Cold War
This topic has no relevance to understanding Canadian history

subjects.subject: English
subjects.title: Literature: Frame Narratives and Nested Stories
subjects.summary: Grade 10 English strand: a frame narrative is a story structure in which one story is told within another, using an outer narrative to introduce, contextualize, or provide a lens for one or more inner stories.

quiz.q: A frame narrative is best described as a structure in which ___.
options: A concept unrelated to narrative structure
Every character narrates the exact same events identically
Only a single storyline exists with no embedded stories
One story is told within another

quiz.q: In a frame narrative, the outer story typically functions to ___.
options: Introduce, contextualize, or provide a lens for the inner story or stories
A function unrelated to frame narratives
Replace the need for an inner story entirely
Have no relationship to the inner story at all

quiz.q: Which is an example of how a frame narrative might be used?
options: A technique unrelated to frame narratives
A story told entirely without any narrator
A character recounting a past experience as a story within the main narrative
A single event described only once with no embedded structure

quiz.q: Why might an author use a frame narrative to explore a theme from multiple angles?
options: Frame narratives never provide any additional perspective on a theme
The relationship between the outer and inner stories can add layered perspectives or meaning to a shared theme
A reason unrelated to narrative structure
A frame narrative always simplifies a theme rather than layering it

quiz.q: Why can a frame narrative create suspense or curiosity about how the outer and inner stories connect?
options: The outer and inner stories are always completely unrelated with no connection at all
Readers may wonder how the framing story relates to or is resolved by the story nested within it
A reason unrelated to narrative technique
Frame narratives never create any sense of suspense or curiosity

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Algebra: Laws of Exponents and Rational Exponents
subjects.summary: Grade 10 Algebra strand (extension): the laws of exponents govern how expressions with powers are multiplied, divided, and simplified, and a rational exponent expresses a root, such as x^(1/2) representing the square root of x.

quiz.q: According to the product law of exponents, x^a multiplied by x^b equals ___.
options: x^(a+b)
An expression unrelated to the laws of exponents
x^(a times b)
x^(a-b)

quiz.q: A rational exponent such as x^(1/2) represents ___.
options: A concept unrelated to rational exponents
X multiplied by one-half
The square root of x
X raised to a negative power

quiz.q: According to the quotient law of exponents, x^a divided by x^b equals ___.
options: An expression unrelated to the laws of exponents
x^(a divided by b)
x^(a-b)
x^(a+b)

quiz.q: Why does raising a power to another power require multiplying the exponents, as in (x^a)^b = x^(ab)?
options: Raising a power to another power means repeating the multiplication of x^a a total of b times, which adds the exponent a to itself b times
A reason unrelated to the laws of exponents
This law only applies when a and b are both equal to one
Raising a power to another power always requires adding the exponents instead of multiplying them

quiz.q: Why are rational exponents useful for representing roots within algebraic expressions?
options: They allow roots to be manipulated using the same laws of exponents as whole-number powers
A reason unrelated to simplifying algebraic expressions
Roots can never be expressed using exponents of any kind
Rational exponents have no connection to roots or radicals

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Biology: Biodiversity and Conservation Strategies
subjects.summary: Grade 10 Biology strand: biodiversity refers to the variety of living organisms within an ecosystem, and conservation strategies, such as protected areas and species reintroduction programs, aim to maintain or restore that variety in the face of habitat loss and other threats.

quiz.q: Biodiversity refers to the ___ within an ecosystem.
options: A concept unrelated to ecosystems
Total mass of a single dominant species
Variety of living organisms
Amount of available sunlight only

quiz.q: Which of these is an example of a conservation strategy?
options: Establishing a protected area to preserve a habitat
Removing all protections from a natural habitat
A strategy unrelated to conservation
Deliberately introducing an invasive species to an ecosystem

quiz.q: Habitat loss is generally considered a ___ to biodiversity.
options: Threat
A factor with no measurable effect on biodiversity
A factor unrelated to biodiversity
Benefit

quiz.q: Why is high biodiversity generally considered beneficial to the overall stability of an ecosystem?
options: High biodiversity always makes an ecosystem less stable
Biodiversity has no connection to how an ecosystem responds to change
A greater variety of species can make an ecosystem more resilient to disturbances or environmental change
A reason unrelated to ecosystem stability

quiz.q: Why might a species reintroduction program be used as a conservation strategy?
options: Species that are lost from an ecosystem can never be reintroduced
It can help restore a species that has been lost from an ecosystem, supporting the ecosystem’s overall balance and function
Reintroduction programs have no effect on an ecosystem’s balance
A reason unrelated to conservation biology

subjects.subject: History
subjects.title: The Massey Commission and the Shaping of Canadian Culture
subjects.summary: Grade 10 History strand: the 1949-1951 Massey Commission investigated the state of arts, letters, and sciences in Canada, leading to recommendations that shaped the creation of institutions such as the Canada Council for the Arts to support Canadian cultural identity.

quiz.q: The Massey Commission conducted its investigation primarily during which years?
The Massey Commission investigated the state of ___ in Canada.
options: Provincial boundaries and geography
Arts, letters, and sciences
Only military spending, with no connection to culture
A subject entirely unrelated to Canadian culture

quiz.q: Recommendations from the Massey Commission helped lead to the creation of ___.
options: The Canadian Armed Forces
A federal department focused only on agriculture
The Canada Council for the Arts
An organization entirely unrelated to Canadian culture

quiz.q: Why was the Massey Commission concerned about the influence of American media and culture on Canada?
options: This topic has no relevance to understanding Canadian history
The Massey Commission had no concerns about American cultural influence
This concern has no connection to Canadian cultural policy
Commissioners worried that a lack of support for Canadian arts and culture could weaken a distinct Canadian identity

quiz.q: Why do historians consider the Massey Commission significant in the development of Canadian cultural policy?
options: It laid the groundwork for ongoing federal support of Canadian arts and culture that continues to shape national identity today
The Massey Commission had no lasting influence on Canadian cultural policy
Canadian cultural policy has never been shaped by federal government action
This topic has no relevance to understanding Canadian history

subjects.subject: English
subjects.title: Oral Communication: Giving and Receiving Constructive Feedback
subjects.summary: Grade 10 English strand: constructive feedback offers specific, actionable observations intended to help a writer or speaker improve, balancing honest critique with respect and encouragement.

quiz.q: Constructive feedback is best described as feedback that is ___.
options: Intended only to criticize without any purpose of improvement
A concept unrelated to communication
Specific, actionable, and intended to help someone improve
Vague and focused only on general praise with no detail

quiz.q: Why is specificity important when giving feedback on a piece of writing?
options: A reason unrelated to giving effective feedback
Specific feedback helps the writer understand exactly what to revise and how to improve it
Vague feedback is always more helpful than specific feedback
Specificity has no effect on how useful feedback is

quiz.q: Which is an example of constructive feedback on a piece of writing?
options: A comment unrelated to giving feedback
I have no comments at all.
Your introduction could more clearly state your main argument.
This is bad.

quiz.q: Why might a peer feedback session use a structured format, such as identifying one strength and one area for improvement?
options: Structured formats always make feedback less useful
A reason unrelated to peer feedback
A structured format ensures feedback is balanced, focused, and useful for revision
Feedback sessions are more effective with no structure at all

quiz.q: Why is learning to receive feedback gracefully considered an important part of the writing process?
options: A reason unrelated to the writing process
Being open to feedback allows a writer to revise and strengthen their work more effectively
Receiving feedback has no connection to improving a piece of writing
Writers should always ignore any feedback they receive

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Vectors: The Dot Product and Applications
subjects.summary: Grade 10 Vectors strand (extension): the dot product combines two vectors to produce a single scalar value, calculated by multiplying corresponding components and summing the results, and can be used to determine the angle between two vectors.

quiz.q: The dot product of two vectors produces a ___.
options: A new vector, with no connection to a scalar
A concept unrelated to vectors
A matrix, with no connection to scalars
Single scalar value

quiz.q: The dot product of two vectors is calculated by ___.
options: Subtracting corresponding components from one another
Multiplying only the magnitudes of the two vectors, ignoring direction
A method unrelated to the dot product
Multiplying corresponding components and summing the results

quiz.q: If the dot product of two non-zero vectors equals zero, the vectors are ___.
options: Perpendicular to each other
A relationship unrelated to the dot product
Parallel to each other
Identical in both magnitude and direction

quiz.q: Why can the dot product be used to determine the angle between two vectors?
options: The dot product formula directly relates the vectors’ magnitudes and the cosine of the angle between them
A reason unrelated to vector operations
The angle between two vectors can never be calculated using any vector operation
The dot product has no connection to the angle between two vectors

quiz.q: Why is the dot product useful in real-world applications, such as calculating the work done by a force?
options: Work done by a force never involves any relationship between two vectors
The dot product has no real-world applications
A reason unrelated to vector operations
It captures how much one vector, like a force, acts in the direction of another, like displacement

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Physics: Simple Machines and Mechanical Advantage
subjects.summary: Grade 10 Physics strand: a simple machine, such as a lever or pulley, changes the direction or magnitude of an applied force, and mechanical advantage measures how much a machine multiplies an input force.

quiz.q: A simple machine changes the direction or magnitude of ___.
options: The colour of an object
An applied force
The mass of an object, with no connection to force
A concept unrelated to simple machines

quiz.q: Mechanical advantage measures how much a machine ___.
options: Reduces the total energy in a system
Multiplies an input force
Changes the temperature of an object
A concept unrelated to mechanical advantage

quiz.q: Which of these is an example of a simple machine?
options: A device unrelated to simple machines
A lever
A smartphone
A television

quiz.q: Why does using a lever allow a person to lift a heavier load with less applied force?
options: A reason unrelated to simple machines
Levers have no effect on the amount of force needed to lift a load
A lever always requires the exact same amount of force as lifting the load directly
The lever multiplies the applied force by trading off distance, requiring the effort to move a greater distance than the load

quiz.q: Why are simple machines considered fundamental to understanding more complex mechanical systems?
options: Complex mechanical systems never rely on any simple machine principles
Complex machines are often built from combinations of simple machines working together
Simple machines have no connection to more complex mechanical systems
A reason unrelated to physics

subjects.subject: History
subjects.title: Civics: How a Bill Becomes a Law in Canada
subjects.summary: Grade 10 Civics strand: a bill must pass through multiple stages in both the House of Commons and the Senate, including readings, committee review, and votes, before receiving royal assent and becoming law.

quiz.q: A bill must pass through multiple stages in which two bodies before becoming law?
options: Only the Supreme Court, with no connection to Parliament
Only a provincial legislature, with no federal involvement
The House of Commons and the Senate
A pair of bodies unrelated to the legislative process

quiz.q: Before becoming law, a bill must receive ___.
options: Royal assent
A public referendum vote
A step unrelated to the legislative process
Approval from every province individually

quiz.q: Committee review of a bill typically involves ___.
options: A step unrelated to the legislative process
The complete cancellation of the bill in every case
An automatic, unquestioned approval with no examination
Detailed examination and possible amendments to the bill

quiz.q: Why does a bill typically go through multiple readings in the House of Commons before becoming law?
options: Multiple readings serve no purpose in the legislative process
A reason unrelated to how laws are made
Multiple readings allow for debate, scrutiny, and revision before a final decision is made
A bill only ever requires a single reading with no further discussion

quiz.q: Why is understanding the legislative process considered an important part of Canadian civics education?
options: This knowledge has no relevance to being an informed citizen
It helps citizens understand how laws affecting their lives are debated, revised, and ultimately passed
This topic has no relevance to understanding Canadian government
The legislative process has no connection to laws that affect citizens

subjects.subject: English
subjects.title: Reading: Analyzing Dialogue as Characterization
subjects.summary: Grade 10 English strand: dialogue reveals character through word choice, tone, and speech patterns, allowing readers to infer a character’s personality, background, and relationships without direct narrator description.

quiz.q: Dialogue can reveal character through a speaker’s ___.
options: A concept unrelated to characterization
Setting alone, with no connection to how a character speaks
Word choice, tone, and speech patterns
Physical appearance exclusively, with no connection to speech

quiz.q: Why might an author give two characters noticeably different speech patterns?
options: A reason unrelated to characterization through dialogue
Distinct speech patterns can help distinguish their personalities, backgrounds, or social status
Every character in a story must speak in an identical way
Speech patterns never provide any information about a character

quiz.q: Which is an example of how dialogue might reveal a character’s emotional state?
options: A character speaking in short, clipped sentences when they are angry or anxious
Dialogue can only reveal a character’s physical location
A character’s dialogue never changes based on their emotional state
A technique unrelated to characterization

quiz.q: Why is dialogue often considered a more subtle form of characterization than direct narrator description?
options: Dialogue is always more direct and obvious than narrator description
It allows readers to infer a character’s traits themselves, rather than being told directly by the narrator
Readers can never infer anything about a character from dialogue
A reason unrelated to characterization

quiz.q: Why might a character’s relationship with another character be revealed through their dialogue?
options: Relationships between characters can only be shown through narration, never dialogue
A reason unrelated to characterization
The way characters address or respond to one another can reflect the closeness, tension, or power dynamic between them
Dialogue never reveals anything about the relationship between two characters

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Polynomial Functions: Zeros, Factors, and End Behaviour
subjects.summary: Grade 10 Functions strand (extension): the zeros of a polynomial function are the x-values where the function equals zero, directly corresponding to its factors, and a polynomial’s degree and leading coefficient determine its end behaviour.

quiz.q: The zeros of a polynomial function are the x-values where the function’s output equals ___.
options: A value unrelated to finding zeros
The function’s leading coefficient

quiz.q: If (x - 3) is a factor of a polynomial function, then the function must have a zero at ___.
options: A value unrelated to this factor

quiz.q: The end behaviour of a polynomial function is primarily determined by its ___.
options: A concept unrelated to polynomial functions
Degree and leading coefficient
Y-intercept only, with no connection to degree
Constant term only, with no connection to degree

quiz.q: Why does an even-degree polynomial function with a positive leading coefficient rise on both ends of its graph?
options: As x approaches positive or negative infinity, an even power always produces a large positive result, multiplied by the positive coefficient
The leading coefficient has no effect on a polynomial’s end behaviour
A reason unrelated to end behaviour
Even-degree polynomials always fall on both ends of the graph regardless of their leading coefficient

quiz.q: Why is identifying the zeros and factors of a polynomial function useful for sketching its graph?
options: Zeros and factors provide no useful information for graphing a polynomial function
The zeros indicate where the graph crosses or touches the x-axis, helping outline the overall shape of the curve
A polynomial’s graph can never be sketched using its zeros or factors
A reason unrelated to polynomial functions

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Earth Science: Soil Formation and Land Use
subjects.summary: Grade 10 Earth Science strand: soil forms gradually through the weathering of rock and the accumulation of organic matter, and land use decisions, such as farming practices or urban development, can significantly affect soil health and erosion rates.

quiz.q: Soil forms gradually through the weathering of rock and the accumulation of ___.
options: A concept unrelated to soil formation
Organic matter
Metal ore exclusively
Liquid water only, with no connection to organic matter

quiz.q: Which of these is an example of a land use decision that could affect soil health?
options: The distance to the nearest ocean
The average daily temperature of the sun
A factor unrelated to land use
Farming practices

quiz.q: Soil formation is generally considered a ___ process.
options: Instantaneous
Slow, gradual
Process that reverses itself every day
A process unrelated to soil formation

quiz.q: Why can certain farming practices, such as leaving fields bare after harvest, increase the rate of soil erosion?
options: Without plant roots or cover to hold it in place, exposed soil is more easily carried away by wind or water
A reason unrelated to soil erosion
Farming practices have no connection to how quickly soil erodes
Bare soil is always more resistant to erosion than soil covered by plants

quiz.q: Why is understanding soil formation and land use important for sustainable agriculture?
options: Soil formation has no connection to agricultural sustainability
Soil erosion has no effect on a region’s ability to grow food over time
A reason unrelated to Earth Science
Since soil forms so slowly, protecting it from excessive erosion or degradation is essential for long-term food production

subjects.subject: History
subjects.title: Civics: The Canadian Electoral System
subjects.summary: Grade 10 Civics strand: Canada uses a first-past-the-post electoral system, in which the candidate who receives the most votes in a riding wins that seat, and the party that wins the most seats typically forms government.

quiz.q: Canada’s federal electoral system is generally described as ___.
options: A system requiring a majority of 50 percent or more to win a riding
Pure proportional representation
A system unrelated to Canadian elections
First-past-the-post

quiz.q: In a first-past-the-post system, the winning candidate in a riding is the one who receives ___.
options: A result unrelated to this electoral system
The fewest votes among all candidates
Exactly fifty percent of the vote
The most votes, even without an outright majority

quiz.q: The political party that wins the most seats in a federal election typically ___.
options: Is automatically dissolved
Forms government
A result unrelated to Canadian elections
Loses all further involvement in government

quiz.q: Why can a party sometimes form a majority government without winning a majority of the total popular vote nationwide?
options: A majority government always requires more than fifty percent of the national popular vote
This outcome is impossible under Canada’s electoral system
Under first-past-the-post, seats are won riding by riding, so a party can win many ridings without needing an overall majority of votes
A reason unrelated to Canada’s electoral system

quiz.q: Why is understanding the electoral system considered important for informed civic participation?
options: The electoral system has no connection to civic participation
It helps citizens understand how their votes translate into political representation and government formation
Understanding elections has no relevance to being an informed citizen
This topic has no relevance to understanding Canadian government

subjects.subject: English
subjects.title: Grammar: Modifiers and Sentence Clarity
subjects.summary: Grade 10 English strand: a modifier describes, clarifies, or adds detail to another word in a sentence, and a misplaced or dangling modifier can create confusing or unintentionally humorous sentence meanings.

quiz.q: A modifier is best described as a word or phrase that ___.
options: Always replaces the subject of a sentence entirely
Has no grammatical function within a sentence
Describes, clarifies, or adds detail to another word in a sentence
A concept unrelated to sentence structure

quiz.q: A misplaced modifier occurs when ___.
options: A modifier is positioned so that it appears to describe the wrong word in a sentence
A sentence contains no modifiers at all
A modifier is placed directly next to the word it describes
A concept unrelated to grammar

quiz.q: Which of these sentences contains a misplaced modifier?
options: A sentence unrelated to misplaced modifiers
Running down the street, the bus was missed by Sam.
Sam missed the bus while running down the street.
While running down the street, Sam missed the bus.

quiz.q: Why can a dangling modifier create confusion for a reader?
options: A reason unrelated to sentence clarity
Dangling modifiers only ever occur in spoken language, never in writing
Dangling modifiers always make a sentence perfectly clear
It leaves unclear or implies an incorrect subject for the action being described

quiz.q: Why is revising for misplaced and dangling modifiers an important step in the editing process?
options: This type of revision has no effect on a sentence’s clarity
Misplaced modifiers never cause any confusion for a reader
Correcting them ensures a sentence clearly communicates its intended meaning to the reader
A reason unrelated to the editing process

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Geometry: Dilations and Scale Factor
subjects.summary: Grade 10 Geometry strand: a dilation is a transformation that resizes a figure proportionally from a fixed centre point, using a scale factor to determine whether the resulting image is enlarged or reduced.

quiz.q: A dilation is a transformation that ___.
options: Reflects a figure across a line, with no change in size
A concept unrelated to geometric transformations
Moves a figure without changing its size
Resizes a figure proportionally from a fixed centre point

quiz.q: If a dilation has a scale factor greater than 1, the resulting image is ___.
options: Identical in size to the original figure
Enlarged
A result unrelated to scale factor
Reduced

quiz.q: If a dilation has a scale factor between 0 and 1, the resulting image is ___.
options: Enlarged
A result unrelated to scale factor
Reflected across the centre point
Reduced

quiz.q: Why do the angles of a figure remain unchanged after a dilation, even though its side lengths change?
options: A dilation resizes a figure proportionally, preserving its overall shape and therefore its angle measures
Angles always change proportionally along with the side lengths during a dilation
Dilations never preserve any properties of the original figure
A reason unrelated to dilations

quiz.q: Why are dilations and scale factor useful concepts in real-world contexts, such as creating a scale model or a map?
options: They allow a real object to be proportionally resized while maintaining its accurate overall shape
A reason unrelated to geometric transformations
Scale models and maps never involve any proportional resizing of a real object
Dilations and scale factor have no real-world modelling applications

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Biology: Plant Structures and Transport Systems
subjects.summary: Grade 10 Biology strand: vascular plants use specialized tissues called xylem and phloem to transport water, minerals, and the products of photosynthesis throughout the plant.

quiz.q: Xylem tissue in a plant is primarily responsible for transporting ___.
options: Oxygen exclusively, with no connection to water or minerals
Only the products of photosynthesis, with no connection to water
Water and minerals
A concept unrelated to plant transport systems

quiz.q: Phloem tissue in a plant is primarily responsible for transporting ___.
options: A concept unrelated to plant transport systems
Only water, with no connection to sugars
The products of photosynthesis, such as sugars
Carbon dioxide exclusively

quiz.q: In most plants, water generally moves through the xylem in which direction?
options: A direction unrelated to xylem transport
Downward, from the leaves toward the roots
Sideways only, with no vertical movement
Upward, from the roots toward the leaves

quiz.q: Why is it useful for a plant to have two separate transport tissues, xylem and phloem, rather than just one?
options: A reason unrelated to plant biology
Xylem and phloem always transport the exact same substances in the exact same direction
Plants have no need to transport any substances internally
Each tissue is specialized to move different substances, such as water versus sugars, often in different directions at once

quiz.q: Why are xylem and phloem considered essential to the survival of tall vascular plants, like trees?
options: A reason unrelated to plant biology
Tall plants have no need to transport water or nutrients internally
They allow water, nutrients, and sugars to be efficiently transported over long distances between the roots and the leaves
Xylem and phloem have no connection to how tall a plant can grow

subjects.subject: History
subjects.title: Civics: Levels of Government — Federal, Provincial, and Municipal
subjects.summary: Grade 10 Civics strand: Canada’s system of government is divided into federal, provincial, and municipal levels, each responsible for distinct areas such as national defense, education and healthcare, and local services like garbage collection and zoning.

quiz.q: Canada’s system of government is divided into which three levels?
options: Federal, provincial, and municipal
A set of levels unrelated to Canadian government
National, international, and local only
Executive, legislative, and judicial only, with no connection to jurisdiction

quiz.q: Which level of government is typically responsible for national defense?
options: A level unrelated to this responsibility
Federal
Municipal
Provincial

quiz.q: Which level of government is typically responsible for local services like garbage collection and zoning?
options: Municipal
An international governing body
A level unrelated to this responsibility
Federal

quiz.q: Why are education and healthcare typically managed primarily at the provincial level in Canada?
options: A reason unrelated to how Canadian government responsibilities are divided
The provincial level of government has no defined responsibilities
Canada’s constitution assigns these areas of responsibility to provincial governments rather than the federal government
Education and healthcare are always managed exclusively at the federal level

quiz.q: Why is understanding the division of responsibilities across levels of government important for Canadian citizens?
options: It helps citizens know which level of government to contact or hold accountable for a specific issue
This topic has no relevance to understanding Canadian civics
This division of responsibilities has no relevance to Canadian citizens
All levels of government are responsible for identical issues

subjects.subject: English
subjects.title: Review: Narrative Technique, Style, and Professional Writing
subjects.summary: Grade 10 English strand: this review lesson revisits the unreliable narrator, motif and recurring imagery, sentence fragments and run-ons, diction and tone, the cover letter and resume, frame narratives, constructive feedback, dialogue as characterization, and modifiers covered across Days 71-79.

quiz.q: An unreliable narrator is best described as a narrator whose ___.
options: A concept unrelated to narrative point of view
Credibility or accuracy is compromised in some way
Account is always completely accurate and objective
Only appears in non-fiction texts, never in fiction

quiz.q: A motif is best described as a recurring ___.
options: A concept unrelated to literary analysis
Character who appears only once in a story
Element, image, or idea that appears throughout a text
Setting that changes completely with every scene

quiz.q: A resume is best described as a document that ___.
options: Tells a detailed personal story with no organization
A concept unrelated to professional writing
Summarizes a candidate’s skills, education, and experience in a clear, organized format
Is always written in the same format as a cover letter

quiz.q: Dialogue can reveal character through a speaker’s ___.
options: Setting alone, with no connection to how a character speaks
Physical appearance exclusively, with no connection to speech
A concept unrelated to characterization
Word choice, tone, and speech patterns

quiz.q: Why is it useful to review these varied narrative and professional writing skills together?
options: These topics have no connection to each other
It reinforces how narrative technique, style, and clear communication all shape effective writing
Each topic must be studied with no connection to the others
Review is never useful in English studies

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Review: Discriminants, Variation, Finance, and Vectors
subjects.summary: Grade 10 Math strand review: this lesson revisits the discriminant, direct and partial variation, compound interest, angle of elevation and depression, the three-dimensional Pythagorean theorem, laws of exponents, the dot product, polynomial zeros and factors, and dilations covered across Days 71-79.

quiz.q: The discriminant of a quadratic equation is calculated using the expression ___.
options: An expression unrelated to the discriminant
b² + 4ac
b² - 4ac
-b divided by 2a

quiz.q: Direct variation describes a relationship expressed in the form ___.
options: y = k divided by x
y = kx
An expression unrelated to direct variation
y = kx + b

quiz.q: Compound interest is calculated based on ___.
options: Both the initial principal and the accumulated interest from previous periods
Only the initial principal, with no connection to accumulated interest
A concept unrelated to interest calculations
A fixed amount that never changes over time

quiz.q: If the dot product of two non-zero vectors equals zero, the vectors are ___.
options: Identical in both magnitude and direction
A relationship unrelated to the dot product
Parallel to each other
Perpendicular to each other

quiz.q: Why is it valuable to review these connected mathematical concepts together?
options: Review is never useful in math
These concepts have no connection to each other
Each concept must be understood with no connection to the others
It reinforces how these skills build on and relate to one another

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Review: Periodic Trends, Body Systems, Energy, and Earth Resources
subjects.summary: Grade 10 Science strand review: this lesson revisits periodic trends, the circulatory and respiratory systems, static electricity, renewable and non-renewable energy resources, empirical and molecular formulas, biodiversity and conservation, simple machines, soil formation, and xylem and phloem covered across Days 71-79.

quiz.q: As you move from left to right across a period, atomic radius generally ___.
options: Increases
A trend unrelated to atomic radius
Stays exactly the same
Decreases

quiz.q: The circulatory system primarily transports oxygen, nutrients, and waste using the heart and ___.
options: Blood vessels
Lungs exclusively, with no connection to blood vessels
A structure unrelated to circulation
Digestive organs only, with no connection to blood

quiz.q: Renewable energy resources are best described as resources that ___.
options: Can never be replenished under any circumstances
Can be replenished naturally within a short time frame
Take millions of years to form
A concept unrelated to energy resources

quiz.q: Mechanical advantage measures how much a machine ___.
options: Changes the temperature of an object
A concept unrelated to mechanical advantage
Reduces the total energy in a system
Multiplies an input force

quiz.q: Why is it valuable to review these connected science concepts together?
options: It reinforces how these scientific ideas relate to and build on one another
These concepts have no connection to each other
Review is never useful at the end of a unit
Each concept must be understood with no connection to the others

subjects.subject: History
subjects.title: Review: The World Wars, Autonomy, and Canadian Civics
subjects.summary: Grade 10 History strand review: this lesson revisits Canada and the First World War, the Conscription Crisis, the Chanak Affair and Statute of Westminster, the Spanish Flu pandemic, the Bomarc missile crisis, the Massey Commission, and how bills, elections, and levels of government function, covered across Days 71-79.

quiz.q: Canada entered the First World War in which year?
The Conscription Crisis took place in which year?
The Statute of Westminster was passed in which year?
Canada’s federal electoral system is generally described as ___.
options: First-past-the-post
A system unrelated to Canadian elections
Pure proportional representation
A system requiring a majority of 50 percent or more to win a riding

quiz.q: Why is it valuable to review these connected developments in Canadian history and civics together?
options: Review is never useful in history
These events have no meaningful connections to each other
It reinforces how policy, autonomy, and civic structures have interacted throughout Canadian history
Each event must be studied with no connection to the others
